findings.description:
indent_block uses text.splitlines() then joins with "
", which silently drops any trailing newline in the input and rewrites exotic Unicode line separators (\v,  , \x85,  , 
, etc.) to plain 
, so the function is not newline-preserving despite only claiming to add indentation.

mask_middle reveals almost the entire value just above the masking threshold: for len(value) == visible*2 + 1 it exposes 2*visible chars and masks only the single middle char (e.g. 8 of 9 chars shown with visible=4), so the docstring promise to 'avoid leaking most of the content' holds only at/below the threshold, not just past it (security-flavored; defer detail to critic-security).

slugify does not guard against max_length < 0: a negative limit makes slug[:max_length] drop that many trailing characters instead of returning an empty/short slug, silently corrupting the result rather than rejecting or clamping the bad argument.

mask_middle never validates that mask_char is a single character; passing a multi-char mask_char multiplies it (mask_char * n), changing the output length and breaking the implied 'same length, middle masked' shape.
indent_block silently no-ops on a negative spaces value (" " * -2 == "") instead of raising, so a caller passing a bad indent gets unindented output with no signal.
